## Summary

<!-- What does this change and why? -->

## Spec file(s) touched

<!-- e.g. spec/grammar.md, profiles/gdpr/v1.md -->

## Change type

- [ ] Editorial (typo, clarification, example, conformance vector)
- [ ] Normative (changes conforming behavior — requires an accepted RFC; link it)

## Checklist

- [ ] Commits are signed off (`git commit -s`) per the DCO in `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [ ] If normative, an RFC is linked and accepted
- [ ] Relative links resolve and examples are valid
